title: Finetuning-Free Diffusion Model with Adaptive Constraint Guidance for Inorganic Crystal Structure Generation
abstract: arXiv:2604.13354v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Generative diffusion models have emerged as powerful tools for the discovery of inorganic crystal structures, yet steering their sampling process toward user-defined physical and chemical objectives remains challenging. We present a computational framework that integrates adaptive constraint guidance into a pre-trained crystal diffusion model, enabling the generation of candidate structures that satisfy targeted structural and chemical requirements without model retraining. The approach incorporates differentiable constraint functions directly during sampling, providing an interpretable mechanism for expert-driven exploration of the crystal structure space. To assess the reliability of generated candidates, we introduce a multi-stage validation workflow combining descriptor-based analysis, duplicate removal, comparison with reference crystal databases, graph neural network energy prediction, and thermodynamic stability evaluati

Title: Finetuning-Free Diffusion Model with Adaptive Constraint Guidance for Inorganic Crystal Structure Generation
Abstract: arXiv:2604.13354v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: Generative diffusion models have emerged as powerful tools for the discovery of inorganic crystal structures, yet steering their sampling process toward user-defined physical and chemical objectives remains challenging. We present a computational framework that integrates adaptive constraint guidance into a pre-trained crystal diffusion model, enabling the generation of candidate structures that satisfy targeted structural and chemical requirements without model retraining. The approach incorporates differentiable constraint functions directly during sampling, providing an interpretable mechanism for expert-driven exploration of the crystal structure space. To assess the reliability of generated candidates, we introduce a multi-stage validation workflow combining descriptor-based analysis, duplicate removal, comparison with reference crystal databases, graph neural network energy prediction, and thermodynamic stability evaluati
translated_title: 无需微调的自适应约束引导扩散模型用于无机晶体结构生成
translated_abstract: 摘要：生成式扩散模型已成为发现无机晶体结构的强大工具，然而，如何引导其采样过程以满足用户定义的物理和化学目标仍具挑战性。我们提出了一种计算框架，将自适应约束引导集成到预训练的晶体扩散模型中，无需重新训练模型即可生成满足特定结构和化学要求的候选结构。该方法在采样过程中直接引入可微的约束函数，为专家驱动的晶体结构空间探索提供了一种可解释的机制。为了评估生成候选结构的可靠性，我们引入了一个多阶段验证流程，结合了基于描述符的分析、重复结构去除、与参考晶体数据库的比对、图神经网络能量预测以及热力学稳定性评估。
tldr: 提出了一种无需重新训练模型的自适应约束引导框架，通过集成可微约束函数，使预训练晶体扩散模型能直接生成满足用户指定物理化学要求的候选结构，并配套多阶段验证流程确保可靠性。
en_tdlr: This work proposes a finetuning-free framework that integrates adaptive constraint guidance into a pre-trained crystal diffusion model, enabling direct generation of candidate structures meeting user-specified physicochemical requirements via differentiable constraint functions, coupled with a multi-stage validation workflow for reliability assessment.
